title: House
description: One house record (catalog product or building documentation). Enum values must come from data/ontology.json.

id.description: Globally unique numeric id. The on-disk key is `house-<id>`.

model.description: Human-readable name. For catalog records, the model/variant name; for documentation records, the building's address or descriptor.

source.description: Where the record came from. Enum: see ontology.sources.

source_origin.description: Free-form note on the source documents (e.g. 'scanned 1956 Baubeschreibung + Ansichten').

year_built.description: Year of original construction (existing buildings) or product introduction (catalog).

levels.description: Stack of inhabited levels, top-to-bottom in build order, e.g. ['KG','EG','OG','DG']. Enum values: see ontology.levels.

agent_notes.description: Hinweise für AI-Agenten, die das Haus modellieren.

images.description: Per-image metadata. Empty array if no images yet.

source_pdfs.description: List of source-PDF filenames in the house folder (auto-discovered if omitted).

derived_facts.description: House-level numeric facts derived + cross-checked from per-scene facts. Each entry is {value, sources: [scene-file], expected?, ok?}. Produced by the house-data-standardization skill.

sources.description: Image filenames (scenes) the value was derived from.

expected.description: Optional expected value / range for cross-check.

ok.description: Did the derived value match the expected?

anomaly_flags.description: House-level data inconsistencies surfaced during standardization (e.g. 'scene-date 1939 contradicts character text saying 1956'). Human review required.

bim_ai_blocking_issues.description: Tri-state: field missing OR null → not yet assessed (UI shows no badge). Empty list → assessed, no blockers (✓ modellierbar). Non-empty list → blocked by these issues until they close. Format: 'owner/repo#NNN'. Cache in data/.issue_state.json, refresh via `make refresh-issue-state`.

data_quality.description: How reconstructable the house is from THIS entry alone. The API derives a single `reconstructability_tier` from these fields (rolled up, not stored on disk). Most axes can be auto-populated by scripts/derive_data_quality.py from the images array; upgrading past 'dimensioned' / 'summary' requires reading the source.

file.description: Filename relative to the house folder (e.g. 'hanse_variant35172_exterior10.original.avif').

view.description: Enum: see ontology.image_views. Only meaningful for exteriors/elevations.

floor.description: Enum: see ontology.levels. Only meaningful for floorplans.

source_ref.description: Provenance: which source artifact this scene was cropped/rendered from. Empty for original (non-derived) images. Populated by the house-data-standardization skill.

file.description: Source filename relative to the house folder (e.g. 'Ansichten.pdf' or 'mhstyle_floorplan1.original.avif').

page.description: PDF page number (1-indexed). Null for non-PDF sources.

crop_box_pct.description: [x0, y0, x1, y1] as fractions of the source-page dimensions (DPI-independent).

dpi.description: Render DPI for the source PDF page when reconstructing the scene image. Optional; the renderer defaults to 200 if omitted.

page_title.description: Title block / page header text observed on the source page.

facts.description: Scene-level numeric/categorical facts extracted by AI vision. Each entry is {value, evidence?}; value can be number, string, array, or object.

evidence.description: What in the scene supports this (label text, count rationale, etc.).

anomaly_flags.description: Scene-level anomalies — e.g. 'scale label illegible', 'dimension annotation appears to contradict adjacent label'.
